name: Clackamas County Bank
description_short: Community bank serving Clackamas County, Oregon since 1911, offering personal and business checking, savings, CDs, loans, and digital banking services.
description_long:
Clackamas County Bank has operated as a community financial institution since 1911, establishing itself as a local presence in the Portland metropolitan area. The bank serves both personal and business customers across four physical locations in Sandy, Hoodland, Boring, and Gresham, Oregon.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of retail banking products including personal and business checking accounts, savings accounts, certificates of deposit (CDs), and lending services. Their digital offerings include 24-hour online banking with bill payment capabilities, mobile banking via their CCB Mobile Banking App, mobile check deposit, e-statements, and text banking alerts. Bill payment includes the first 10 monthly payments free with additional payments at $0.55 each. They also feature a Lucky Rewards program tied to debit card usage.

Clackamas County Bank distinguishes itself through its long community history, local branch presence in smaller Oregon communities, and integrated digital services that allow customers to manage accounts via multiple channels. The bank emphasizes accessibility through multiple contact methods, including a dedicated online banking support line (844-445-8225), and provides financial calculators and educational resources on their website.

As a traditional community bank, Clackamas County Bank is best suited for customers who value local branch access, personalized service, and community connection alongside digital convenience. The website content is limited regarding specific account fees, interest rates, loan terms, and minimum balance requirements, which are typical considerations for prospective customers evaluating banking relationships. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Bill payment service ($0.55 per transaction after 10 free monthly payments)
Mobile banking app with check deposit, balance checks, and transfers
pros: Mobile check deposit available through free CCB Mobile Banking App—deposit checks without visiting a branch
Four physical branch locations across Clackamas County for in-person service
24-hour online banking with account access, check images, and transaction history
First 10 bill payments per month included free through online bill pay service
Free mobile banking app with balance checks, fund transfers, and ATM locator
Long operational history since 1911 with established community presence
Debit card rewards program (Lucky Rewards) for everyday purchases
Free e-statements with 24-month online access and downloadable option
cons: Website lacks specific information on checking account fees, minimum balances, and interest rates
Limited loan product details—no specific rates, terms, or eligibility criteria disclosed online
Smaller bank may have fewer ATM locations than national or larger regional banks
No mention of online account opening—may require in-person visit to open accounts
Additional bill payment fee of $0.55 per transaction beyond 10 free monthly payments
best_for: Clackamas County residents seeking local branch service and community banking relationships
Small business owners needing business checking and localized loan services
Digital-savvy customers who want mobile banking convenience with physical branch backup
Customers seeking to support local community financial institutions
diagnosis: Clackamas County Bank is best for Oregon residents in the Clackamas County area who prefer local community banking with established relationships and digital convenience through mobile and online platforms. Primary caveat: the website provides minimal transparency on specific account fees, interest rates, and loan terms, requiring direct contact with branches for detailed pricing information before opening accounts.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
